L1.area: Configuration and default settings for the document conversion pipeline

L2.description: A code injection vulnerability exists where user-supplied document content containing specific delimiter syntax causes the underlying front-matter parsing library to evaluate the content as executable code. An attacker can craft a malicious input document that triggers arbitrary code execution in the conversion process, potentially allowing remote code execution on the server.

L3.area: Default configuration for the front-matter parsing engine settings in the document conversion pipeline
L3.description: A code injection vulnerability exists where a document with a specific front-matter delimiter triggers the underlying parsing library's JavaScript evaluation engine. The default configuration intended to disable this engine does not cover all supported delimiter variants, allowing an attacker to bypass the restriction and execute arbitrary JavaScript code during document conversion.

ground_truth.reason: The default configuration for the gray-matter front-matter parser only disables the 'js' engine variant but not the 'javascript' variant, allowing attacker-controlled Markdown documents with the '---javascript' delimiter to execute arbitrary JavaScript code during conversion, leading to remote code execution.
